text:
opinion|Letters to the Editor

ByLetters to the Editor



Re: “Fire hits historic church — Blaze at First Baptist Dallas causes secondary chapel to largely collapse,” July 20 news story.

I moved to Dallas after graduating from law school in 1980 and I’ve been a member at First Baptist Dallas for 44 years. It was a surreal experience as I watched the fire destroy most of the historic sanctuary.

Like fellow church members, my memory banks are full of countless events that I have experienced with my family in the sanctuary over the years. Most of the building may have been destroyed, but the building’s foundation and the memories remain intact.

Many consider the fire to be a tragedy and while it was a loss, we as Christians know that God is in control. In times like this we must lean on his sovereignty. I trust that he had a purpose in allowing the fire to occur, and he has a plan for our church.

Get smart opinions on the topics North Texans care about.

Or with:

By signing up, you agree to ourTerms of ServiceandPrivacy Policy.

I believe that the best days in the 150-plus years of First Baptist Dallas are yet to come. I also want to express my gratitude to the brave firefighters who tirelessly worked to get the fire under control and extinguished it, as well as the law enforcement officers who kept everyone safe.

Michael Deahl, East Dallas

Re: “Netanyahu vows to reach ‘total victory’ — Fiery speech to Congress prompts boycott, protests,” Thursday news story.

Benjamin Netanyahu is 100% correct. People who may have the best intentions and kindest hearts and are supporting the Palestinians are nothing more than “useful idiots” to Hamas and Iran. The kind hearts have not bothered to do any research into the area or its people.

An early December 2023 poll showed strong Palestinian support for Hamas, which means they also support the Hamas mission statement of genocide against the Jewish people and the destruction of the Israeli state.

It is this overt support by the Palestinian people that allows for embedded terrorists and for military bases to be constructed under civilian structures like hospitals and schools, and for terrorist operations behind civilian shields.

Without popular support, these actions would not and could not occur. A new Reuters poll shows that two-thirds  of Palestinians say the Oct. 7 attack on Israel was justified and “correct.”

The Palestinians have been offered their own state five times: in 1937, 1947, 1967, 2000 and in 2008. However, since it seems their true goal is the destruction of Israel, they will never accept their own separate state.

Wake up, “useful idiots!”

Olan Knight, Murphy

Thank you for publishing this story about Benjamin Netanyahu vowing to reach “total victory” because it exposed the protesters whose pro-Palestinian agenda outranks their patriotism by lowering, in front of Union Station, the American flag and replacing it with a pro-Palestinian one.

If you are dedicated to the cause of the Palestinian people, then pay attention to the anti-American agenda that is lurking within it. Do not turn a blind eye because you don’t want to see such hate for our country. Look at it square in the eye and ask yourself how much anti-American sentiment you are willing to tolerate as part of supporting the pro-Palestinian cause.

Nancy Reuben Greenfield, North Dallas

Re: “What they said,” July 21 Opinion.

I believe you missed the mark in your quest to continuously improve your paper’s truthful and balanced journalistic standards by quoting Sen. Ted Cruz at the Republican National Convention when he said, “Democrats cynically decided they wanted votes from illegals more than they wanted to protect our children.”

That sounds bad, but it also doesn’t make a lick of sense in that undocumented immigrants cannot vote and statistics show a lower crime rate among these immigrants than the population as a whole.

Even though the column is about quotations, whatThe Dallas Morning Newschooses to print has the potential to sway the public, especially if what is said is left unchecked.

Linda Johnston Arage, Waxahachie

Re: “Sorority sister: This is Kamala Harris’ moment — Alpha Kappa Alpha is rooted in service and will mobilize voters for Election Day,” by Danette Anthony Reed, Thursday Opinion.

I was very excited to read this column and to learn of the mobilization of so many like-minded women. Organizations like Reed’s and the women who power them are essential to the dramatic changes that must take place across our social and political landscapes.

Ms. Reed, you have given me heart that we can reclaim, renew and redefine what was hard-fought and won during the last 50 years (and stripped within in the last several).

Laura Lerond, Houston

We welcome your thoughts in a letter to the editor. See the guidelines andsubmit your letter here. If you have problems with the form, you can submit via email atletters@dallasnews.com

title: Letters to the Editor — First Baptist, Israeli PM visit, Alpha Kappa Alpha, Olympics
